creator: nimaje+fbz@bureaucracy.de
text: There is no such thing as a "negative nan", just some nan representation where the signbit is set and some where it is not, but that should be just a nan for every purpose. I would say not printing a sign there is clearer, as nobody can be confused that something like a "negative nan" exists. And that   sign = '\0';   in line 788 seems to be an explicite design desicion to do that to me.
